gene_description: 7-Dehydrocholesterol Reductase — enzyme that converts 7-dehydrocholesterol (7-DHC) to cholesterol. When DHCR7 activity is reduced, more 7-DHC is available for UV-mediated vitamin D3 synthesis in the skin. Key switch between cholesterol and vitamin D production.

source.title: DHCR7: A vital enzyme switch between cholesterol and vitamin D production
source.finding: DHCR7 rs12785878 is a strong GWAS determinant of circulating 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels. Variants that reduce DHCR7 activity increase 7-DHC substrate availability for vitamin D synthesis. These variants were evolutionarily favored in Northern European populations as adaptation to lower UV exposure.

GG.effect: Homozygous G carriers have higher DHCR7 activity, directing more 7-dehydrocholesterol toward cholesterol synthesis rather than vitamin D3 production. Lower circulating 25(OH)D levels. Greater susceptibility to vitamin D deficiency, especially in Northern latitudes with limited UV exposure. May also have slightly higher cholesterol.
GG.recommendation: Vitamin D supplementation recommended (1000-2000 IU daily), especially in winter and at higher latitudes. Regular 25(OH)D monitoring. Maximize safe sun exposure. Vitamin D-rich foods (fatty fish, fortified dairy). Higher supplementation doses may be needed to achieve optimal levels.

GT.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate DHCR7 activity and 7-DHC availability for vitamin D production. Moderate vitamin D levels. Some seasonal variation.
GT.recommendation: Moderate vitamin D supplementation (600-1000 IU daily). Monitor 25(OH)D levels seasonally. Adequate sun exposure and dietary vitamin D.

TG.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate DHCR7 activity and 7-DHC availability for vitamin D production. Moderate vitamin D levels. Some seasonal variation.
TG.recommendation: Moderate vitamin D supplementation (600-1000 IU daily). Monitor 25(OH)D levels seasonally. Adequate sun exposure and dietary vitamin D.

TT.effect: Homozygous T carriers have lower DHCR7 activity, leaving more 7-DHC available for UV-mediated vitamin D3 synthesis in skin. Higher circulating 25(OH)D levels. Evolutionarily favorable variant in Northern climates. Naturally better vitamin D status.
TT.recommendation: Favorable vitamin D genetics. Standard sun exposure and dietary vitamin D intake likely sufficient. Supplementation may still be needed at very high latitudes in winter.
